column1: There's no one selling anything here. Try the bartender out front.
column2: 這裡沒人在賣東西啦，去問前面的酒保。

column1: He's too drunk to want anything.
column2: 他醉到啥都不想要了。

column1: He's beyond help, but thirsty.
column2: 沒救了，但還很渴。

column1:
...my only posshhess... hhic!	「…我唯一的家私…呃嗝！
...all I got in the world.\n\nWhat'sha guy like me need witta TV remote control!?"
column2: 「…我這輩子的全部家當。\n\n像我這種人要電視遙控器是要幹嘛啦！？」

column1: Caught ya! Now open the door, then get your nose out of that manual and start playing this game the right way!
column2: 抓到你了！趕快開門，把鼻子從說明書裡拔出來，正經玩這遊戲啦！

column1: This is the sleaziest bar you've ever been in. And you've been in quite a few.
column2: 這是你這輩子去過最下流的酒吧，而你去過的下流酒吧可不少。

column1: You insert a dollar into the juke box, and select a suitably sleazy song.
column2: 你投一塊錢進點唱機，選了首夠下流的歌。

column1: You rap loudly on the naugahyde door. You wonder how many naugas had to give their all just to decorate this sleazehole.
column2: 你大力敲著人造皮門，心想不知道犧牲了多少隻「納霸」才佈置得出這間爛酒吧。

column1: Yeah. Whatsda passwoid?
column2: 「是喔，通關密語咧？」

column1: Scram, dog breath!
column2: 「滾啦，你這張狗臭嘴！」

column1: ...a lotta guys just leave her hanging in a tree somewhere!
column2: 「…很多人乾脆把她掛樹上就不管了！」

column1: ...'cause Thursday's your night in the barrel!
column2: 「…因為禮拜四就是你倒楣挨桶子的份！」

column1: ...just think of the money he saved!
column2: 「…想想他省下多少錢！」

column1: ...and there stood the pig and the cow!
column2: 「…豬跟牛就這樣站在那裡！」

column1: ...dammit Liza, doncha know ya own reflection?
column2: 「…該死的麗莎，你連自己的倒影都不認得啊？」

column1: ...we are coming back this way, aren't we?
column2: 「…我們等一下還會走這條路吧？」

column1: ...You idiot! I said POSSE!!
column2: 「…你這呆瓜！我說的是『民團』啦！！」

column1: ...nobody can eat THAT much ice cream!
column2: 「…沒有人吃得下那麼多冰淇淋啦！」

column1: ...25 bucks, same as in town!
column2: 「…25 塊，跟鎮上同一個價！」

column1: You'd better sit down first.
column2: 你最好先坐下來。

column1: You greet the bartender.\n\n"What'll it be," Lefty responds, "whiskey, wine or beer?"
column2: 你跟酒保打招呼。\n\n「要喝啥，」老左應道，「威士忌、紅酒還是啤酒？」

column1: I'll have a glass of your fine, well whiskey! you tell Lefty.
column2: 「給我來杯你們家上好的散裝威士忌！」你跟老左說。

column1: Gimme a mug o' beer! you order Lefty.
column2: 「給我一杯啤酒！」你跟老左點餐。

column1: That'll be $3.00, please.
column2: 「三塊，麻煩了。」

column1: You flip three bucks onto the counter.
column2: 你把三塊錢拍上吧檯。

column1: I'm presently a little short, you stammer.
column2: 「我…我現在手頭有點緊，」你結巴地說。

column1: Yeah, and not just in the financial department, I bet! he replies, grabbing back your order.
column2: 「是啊，我看緊的不只是荷包吧！」他邊說邊把你的酒搶回去。

column1: You down the beer in your pseudo-macho style, and slam the mug back on the bar.
column2: 你用你那假掰硬漢的姿勢一口乾了啤酒，把杯子重重砸回吧檯。

column1: You delicately sip the wine until it's all gone.\n\nYou tell Lefty, "I find this impudent and sassy, with the slightest hint of impertinence."
column2: 你優雅地一小口一小口把酒喝光。\n\n你跟老左說：「這酒帶點放肆，又有點調皮，還隱隱透著一絲狂妄。」

column1: He gazes at you longingly and moistens his lips!
column2: 他用渴望的眼神看著你，還舔了舔嘴唇！

column1: Suddenly you feel a little woozy.
column2: 忽然你覺得有點頭重腳輕。

column1: This one tastes even better than the last. One more sounds like a good idea.
column2: 這杯比上一杯還讚。再來一杯感覺是個好主意。

column1: Try sitting at the bar; that usually gets Lefty's attention.
column2: 試試坐在吧檯邊，這樣通常能引起老左注意。

column1: It's not good etiquette to drink standing up.
column2: 站著喝酒不太合禮數。

column1: Since you can only balance one open glass of whiskey at a time, you toss this one down immediately.
column2: 既然你一次只能端一杯開口的威士忌，乾脆直接一口乾了。

column1: May I please have a glass of your delicate white zinfandel, sir? you ask Lefty.
column2: 「可以請您給我一杯溫潤的白金芬黛嗎，先生？」你問老左。

column1: Hey, was that your voice?
column2: 嘿，那是你的聲音嗎？

column1: You don't drink the whiskey, but instead, decide to carry it with you wherever you go, precariously balanced in an open shot glass.
column2: 你沒把威士忌喝掉，反而決定端著它到處走，搖搖晃晃地平衡在那只開口的小杯裡。

column1: The whiskey burns its way down your throat. You vow to never again buy a "well" brand.
column2: 威士忌燒得你喉嚨一路發燙。你發誓再也不買散裝的了。

column1: Hold on; you haven't got your last order yet!
column2: 等一下，你上一杯都還沒拿到咧！

column1: You feel a little light-headed.
column2: 你覺得有點暈頭轉向。

column1: Shaking your head, you clear away the cobwebs and are able to once again walk in your standard, studly style.
column2: 你搖搖頭甩開暈眩，總算又能用你那招牌帥氣步伐走路了。

column1: Cram it, clown! you tell the bore on the stool next to you!
column2: 「閉嘴啦，小丑！」你對隔壁高腳椅上的無聊傢伙說！

column1: Same to ya! shouts Lefty.
column2: 「彼此彼此啦！」老左喊道。

column1: It's an antique, left over from "King's Quest III."
column2: 這是古董，是《King's Quest III》留下來的。

column1: Patience, Larry; patience!
column2: 耐心點，賴瑞，耐心點！

column1: The door is locked. And, there is no key.
column2: 門鎖住了，而且沒有鑰匙。

column1: She may not be a great looker, but think of the muscles in that leg.
column2: 她長相不怎樣，但你看看那條腿上的肌肉。

column1: It's just going around in circles...
column2: 只是不斷繞圈子…

column1: ...like your life.
column2: …就跟你的人生一樣。

column1: You don't know much about art, but you do know what you like!
column2: 你不太懂藝術，但你知道自己喜歡啥！

column1: You mean to say you're playing a 3-D adventure game, and all you want is LIGHT beer??
column2: 你是說你在玩 3D 冒險遊戲，結果只想喝淡啤酒？？

column1: They're just sitting at the bar, drinking their livers into oblivion.
column2: 他們就坐在吧檯，把肝喝到報廢。

column1: Sorry, we don't sell them sissy drinks here, says Lefty. "We got whiskey, wine and beer. What do you want?"
column2: 「歹勢，我們這不賣那種娘泡飲料，」老左說。「只有威士忌、紅酒跟啤酒，你要啥？」

column1: My, aren't we the little savage! Didn't your mother teach you anything?
column2: 唉呦，你這野蠻小子！你媽都沒教過你嗎？

column1: Who cares, she replies sarcastically. "Stop bugging me, or I'll have my boy friend take care of you when he gets back from the rest room!"
column2: 「誰在乎啊，」她酸溜溜地回。「別煩我，不然等我男友上完廁所回來，他會好好『招待』你！」

column1: Looks like you're operating at your standard level of charm, Larry.
column2: 看來你又發揮了一貫水準的魅力嘛，賴瑞。

column1: He has nothing to say, but says it continuously.
column2: 他啥都沒說，但一直不停地說。

column1: Hi there, lovely legs! you say sweetly. "My name's Larry; Larry Laffer."
column2: 「嗨，美腿！」你甜言蜜語地說。「我叫賴瑞，賴瑞・拉佛。」

column1: They have no comment.
column2: 他們沒意見。

column1: He's not bothering you now; leave him alone, you big bully!
column2: 他現在又沒煩你，放他一馬啦，你這大惡霸！

column1: Gee, Dad. It's a Wurlitzer!
column2: 哇塞，老爸，這是台 Wurlitzer 唱片機耶！

column1: The peephole is locked from the inside.
column2: 窺視孔從裡面鎖住了。

column1: Boy, are you drunk!
column2: 天啊，你醉茫茫了！

column1: Ya got me!
column2: 「你抓到我啦！」

column1: No, thanks, Lefty replies. "I don't drink."
column2: 「不用了，」老左說。「我不喝酒。」

column1: Ok, says Lefty, "but that'll cost ya $%v90.00!"
column2: 「好啦，」老左說，「不過要花你 $%v90.00 喔！」

column1: The last of the big time spenders, you go down in a blaze of glory, ordering drinks for everybody in the place.
column2: 你像最後一位闊少爺，轟轟烈烈地請全場喝酒，瀟灑落幕。

column1: I'll not help you, says Lefty, "no matter how much you offer me!"
column2: 「我才不幫你，」老左說，「你出多少錢都沒用！」

column1: The naugahyde bounces the hammer back, grazing your scalp but slightly.
column2: 人造皮把鐵鎚彈了回來，只輕輕刮到你的頭皮。

column1: You flip a fiver out on the bar. Lefty grabs it, smiles, and says, "Hey, thanks buddy!
column2: 你拍出一張五塊到吧檯。老左一把抓走，笑著說：「嘿，謝啦老兄！

column1: You don't have enough money!
column2: 你錢不夠啦！

column1: Double down on eleven.
column2: 「十一點，加倍下注。」

column1: If you're short of money, you could always try gambling at the casino.
column2: 「錢不夠？去賭場碰碰運氣啊。」

column1: If you're bored hanging around my place, try hailing a cab outside.
column2: 「在我這兒待膩了？外面攔輛計程車去晃晃。」

column1: Guys tell me I need to clean my rest room walls.
column2: 「常有人跟我說廁所牆壁該洗一洗了。」

column1: He appears to be perverted, twisted and sick!
column2: 他看起來變態、扭曲又病態！

column1: You instantly take a liking to him.
column2: 你立刻喜歡上他了。

column1: The bar's storage room is almost empty. Its most significant feature is a big, mean-looking dude.
column2: 酒吧的儲藏室幾乎是空的，裡頭最顯眼的就是一個高大兇惡的傢伙。

column1: Ya wanna go upstairs? he says in a scholarly voice, "ya gotta pays da price!"
column2: 「要上樓喔？」他裝出一副斯文腔調，「得付錢才行啦！」

column1: $100.00, cash up front, no refunds.
column2: 「一百塊，先付現金，概不退費。」

column1: He certainly has a pleasing manner about him.
column2: 他這人態度還真是討喜。

column1: Thanks, he growls. "Remember, no refunds!"
column2: 「謝啦，」他低吼道，「記住，不退費！」

column1: Evidently no one in the bar wants to play "Knock, knock" with you.
column2: 顯然酒吧裡沒人想陪你玩「敲敲門」的遊戲。

column1: It's the door from the bar. Its far side looks better than this side.
column2: 這是通往酒吧的門，那一邊看起來比這一邊順眼多了。

column1: You don't have enough money.
column2: 你錢不夠。

column1: You like that stuff, eh, boy?
column2: 「喜歡那玩意兒喔，小子？」

column1: The pimp seems vaguely interested, but not enough to leave his post by the stairs.
column2: 皮條客似乎有點興趣，但還不足以讓他離開樓梯口的崗位。

column1: The pimp growls, "You got no business goin' up these stairs, little man!"
column2: 皮條客低吼：「你沒資格上這樓梯，小子！」

column1: It appears you've finally reached the pimp's cultural level.
column2: 看來你總算跟皮條客的文化水準看齊了。

column1: He ignores you. It looks like he's "engrossed" in something right now.
column2: 他不理你，看起來正「全神貫注」在某件事上。

column1:
Oh, John.  "Oh, Marsha."
"OH, John."  "OH, Marsha."
"Oh, JOHN!"  "Oh, MARSHA!"
"OH, JOHN!!" "OH, MARSHA!!"
column2:
「噢，約翰。」 「噢，瑪莎。」
「噢～約翰。」 「噢～瑪莎。」
「噢，約翰！」 「噢，瑪莎！」
「噢，約翰！！」 「噢，瑪莎！！」

column1: ...I'm Al Fartles, and this is my partner, Fed Ames. We just want to tell you how much we appreciate your support...
column2: 「……我是 Al Fartles，這位是我的搭檔 Fed Ames。我們只想告訴你，我們有多感謝你的支持……」

column1: ...and now, ALoHa Productions is proud to present 'The Revenge of the Software Developers' in full color and stereo sound...
column2: 「……現在，ALoHa Productions 榮幸呈現全彩立體聲鉅獻《軟體工程師大復仇》……」

column1: ...while Jim is fighting for his life with the raging female rhinoceros in heat, I'll slip into my tent with the native girl and this pitcher of Mai Tais...
column2: 「……就在 Jim 跟發情母犀牛拼死搏鬥的同時，我要溜進帳篷，跟當地女孩還有這壺邁泰調酒快活一下……」

column1: ...it's a beautiful day in the neighbor...
column2: 「……這是個風和日麗的社……」

column1: ...live from Malaga, California, it's the Cable Sports Network presentation of the Miniature Female Mud Wrestlers 500-kilometer Cross-Country Nude Bicycle Race...
column2: 「……為您從加州馬拉加現場直播，這是有線體育網呈獻的《迷你女子泥漿摔角手 500 公里越野裸體自行車賽》……」

column1: ...And next, Master Piece Theatre presents a special 3-D broadcast of 'Naugahyde Goddesses of Oakhurst!'
column2: 「……接下來，大師劇院為您呈現 3D 特別放送《橡樹城的納霸皮女神！》」

column1: ...oh, baby, yeah. Yeah, I love it when you do that! Ooooooooooh!! That hurts so good, pull it out a little deeper!!!
column2: 「……噢，寶貝，對，就是那樣，我喜歡你這樣弄！噢噢噢噢！！好舒服，再往裡面推深一點！！！」

column1: The knob is broken off. There's no way to turn it.
column2: 旋鈕斷掉了，沒辦法轉動。

column1: It already is!
column2: 它早就開著了啦！

column1: You click the power switch on the remote control.
column2: 你按下遙控器的電源鈕。

column1: You click the channel selector on the remote control.
column2: 你按下遙控器的選台鈕。

column1: Nah. He's having a good time!
column2: 不用啦，他玩得可開心了！

column1: They're boxes, all right.
column2: 就是箱子沒錯。

column1: The remote control seems ineffectual from this angle.
column2: 從這角度看，遙控器好像不太管用。

column1: The TV is off.
column2: 電視是關著的。

column1: Pretty good stuff, eh, boy? laughs the pimp.
column2: 「東西不賴吧，小子？」皮條客笑著說。

column1: No, no!! Anything but that!
column2: 不要，不要啊！！除了那個什麼都行！

column1: Well, now. What's this??
column2: 唉呦，這又是啥玩意兒？？

column1: Slow down. You're gonna blow the punch line!
column2: 慢一點啦，你要搞砸笑點了！

column1: Have a nice lay.
column2: 「祝你有美好的『一夜』。」

column1: He's no rocket scientist!
column2: 他才不是什麼火箭專家咧！

column1: Its screen is blank; much like the pimp's mind.
column2: 螢幕一片空白，就跟皮條客的腦袋一樣。

column1: Get your mind back on the game, Larry!
column2: 把心思拉回遊戲上啦，賴瑞！

column1: What does this look like, the ship's hold in "King's Quest III?"
column2: 這是怎樣，你以為這是《King's Quest III》的船艙嗎？

column1: The walls do a good job of separating the floor from the ceiling.
column2: 這牆倒是稱職地把地板和天花板隔開了。

column1: It's just hanging around.
column2: 它就掛在那兒。

column1: Wait 'til you get upstairs.
column2: 等你上樓再說吧。

column1: He's far too big, and you're far too wimpy.
column2: 他塊頭太大，你又太弱雞了。

column1: Hey! Outta da way!!
column2: 「嘿！閃邊去啦！！」

column1: There is no need to pay; he's pleasantly distracted. Help yourself!
column2: 不用付錢，他正爽爽地分神呢，自己拿吧！

column1: You wonder what palace of earthly delights resides above you.
column2: 你好奇樓上到底藏著什麼人間享樂殿堂。

column1: Another boring
column2: 又一個無聊的

column1: Evidently the remote control's off button is broken.
column2: 顯然遙控器的關閉鈕壞掉了。

column1: Its operation is beyond his mental capacities.
column2: 操作方式超出他的智商範圍。

column1: He's unaffected by the remote control.
column2: 他完全不受遙控器影響。

column1: He's too strong for you, Larry.
column2: 他對你來說太強了，賴瑞。

column1: This is the hooker's seedy bedroom. The bed's a mess, ditto the hooker!
column2: 這是妓女的髒亂臥房。床亂七八糟，妓女也是一個樣！
